examples.title: Bellman-Ford Basic Algorithm
examples.description: Standard Bellman-Ford algorithm to find shortest paths from single source
examples.code:
ALGORITHM BellmanFord(Graph G, Source s)
BEGIN
  // Initialization
  FOR each vertex v IN G.V
  BEGIN
    distance[v] = INFINITY
    predecessor[v] = NULL
  END
  distance[s] = 0

  // Relaxation - V-1 iterations
  FOR i = 1 TO |G.V| - 1
  BEGIN
    FOR each edge (u, v) IN G.E
    BEGIN
      IF distance[u] + weight(u, v) < distance[v] THEN
        distance[v] = distance[u] + weight(u, v)
        predecessor[v] = u
      END
    END
  END

  // Check for negative weight cycles
  FOR each edge (u, v) IN G.E
  BEGIN
    IF distance[u] + weight(u, v) < distance[v] THEN
      RETURN "Negative cycle detected"
    END
  END

  RETURN distance, predecessor
END
examples.explanation: The algorithm initializes distances, performs V-1 relaxations of all edges, then checks for negative cycles in an extra iteration.
